articles.headline: India Unveils National Circular Economy Policy 2025
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the comprehensive National Circular Economy Policy 2025, aiming to transition India from a linear 'take-make-dispose' model to a restorative and regenerative one.
• The policy focuses on reducing waste generation, maximizing resource utilization, and fostering innovation in recycling and reuse technologies.
• Key pillars include extended producer responsibility, development of material recovery facilities, and promotion of eco-design principles across industries.
• It sets ambitious targets for waste reduction in key sectors like plastics, electronics, and construction & demolition waste by 2030.
• Financial incentives and regulatory frameworks are proposed to encourage businesses and consumers to adopt circular practices.

Conservation

articles.headline: Veteran Politician and Lawyer Swaraj Kaushal Passes Away at 73
articles.explainer:
• Swaraj Kaushal, a distinguished lawyer and politician, passed away at the age of 73.
• He served as a Member of Parliament (MP) in the Rajya Sabha from 1998 to 2004, representing Haryana.
• Notably, he held the gubernatorial post as the Governor of Mizoram from 1990 to 1993, becoming one of India's youngest governors at the time.
• Kaushal was a prominent legal mind and was known for his constitutional expertise and parliamentary contributions.
• He was also the husband of late former External Affairs Minister Sushma Swaraj.

Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.

Examine the evolution of the role of Rajya Sabha in India's legislative process.
articles.imageDescription: Portrait of Swaraj Kaushal.
articles.trivia: Swaraj Kaushal was involved in the peace talks with the Mizo National Front (MNF), which led to the Mizoram Peace Accord in 1986.

articles.headline: RBI Keeps Repo Rate Unchanged, Focuses on Inflation Management
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
• This decision comes amidst persistent concerns over retail inflation, which remains above the MPC's comfort zone, despite easing global commodity prices.
• RBI Governor emphasized the need for continued vigilance on inflation, citing potential risks from volatile food prices and global supply chain disruptions.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Economic growth projections were maintained, with robust domestic demand seen as a key driver.

articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee was constituted in 2016 and is mandated to set the policy interest rate to achieve the inflation target.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Maritime Security Dialogue Concludes with Focus on Indo-Pacific Stability
articles.explainer:
• The 3rd India-ASEAN Maritime Security Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, emphasizing enhanced cooperation for regional stability and freedom of navigation in the Indo-Pacific.
• Delegates discussed joint efforts in counter-piracy, search and rescue operations, maritime domain awareness, and combating illegal, unreported, and unregulated (IUU) fishing.
• India reiterated its commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, aligning with its 'Act East' policy and Security and Growth for All in the Region (SAGAR) vision.
• The dialogue also explored avenues for capacity building and technology sharing among ASEAN member states and India.
• A joint statement highlighted the importance of adhering to international law, particularly UNCLOS, for resolving maritime disputes peacefully.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology Demonstrator
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful atmospheric flight test of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator.
• This critical test validates key technologies for an orbital reusable launch vehicle, including autonomous navigation, guidance, and control, as well as hypersonic flight capabilities.
• The RLV-TD program aims to significantly reduce the cost of space access by developing a two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) reusable launch system.
• The demonstrator, named 'Pushpak', performed a precise landing at a designated airstrip after being released from an Indian Air Force helicopter at an altitude of 4.5 km.
• This achievement marks a major step towards India's future space missions, including human spaceflight and advanced satellite deployments.

articles.trivia: The first RLV-TD mission, HEX (Hypersonic Flight Experiment), was successfully conducted by ISRO in 2016.

articles.headline: Parliament Passes Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Bill, 2025
articles.explainer:
• The Parliament successfully passed the Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Bill, 2025, which seeks to decriminalize minor offenses across various laws.
• The bill aims to promote ease of doing business and ease of living by replacing imprisonment with monetary penalties for several petty infractions.
• It proposes amendments to over 42 central Acts administered by 19 Ministries/Departments, including environmental laws, agriculture, and intellectual property.
• The move is intended to reduce the burden on the judicial system and encourage compliance by shifting from punitive to a more facilitative regulatory regime.
• The Bill also introduces a system of adjudicating officers for these monetary penalties.

articles.trivia: The first version of the Jan Vishwas Bill was introduced in December 2022 and referred to a Joint Committee of Parliament.

articles.headline: India's Solar Energy Capacity Crosses 150 GW Mark, Pushing Towards Green Goals
articles.explainer:
• India's total installed solar power capacity has officially surpassed the 150 GW milestone, marking a significant achievement in its renewable energy transition.
• This includes utility-scale solar projects, rooftop solar installations, and off-grid solar solutions, demonstrating a diversified growth strategy.
• The surge in capacity is attributed to supportive government policies, incentives for solar manufacturing, and declining costs of solar technology.
• This development brings India closer to its ambitious target of 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030, pledged at COP26.
• The growth in solar energy is also contributing to energy security, reduced carbon emissions, and job creation in the green sector.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Constitutional Validity of Reservations in Promotions for SC/STs
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark judgment, reaffirmed the constitutional validity of providing reservations in promotions for Scheduled Castes (SCs) and Scheduled Tribes (STs) in government jobs.
• The five-judge Constitution Bench clarified that while states are not bound to provide reservations in promotions, if they choose to do so, they must collect quantifiable data demonstrating the inadequacy of representation of SC/STs.
• The Court reiterated that the 'creamy layer' concept is not applicable to SC/ST reservations, distinguishing it from OBC reservations.
• This ruling provides much-needed clarity on the contentious issue, aligning with previous judgments while emphasizing the need for data-backed policy decisions.
• The judgment underscores the state's power to make special provisions for the advancement of socially and educationally backward classes.

articles.trivia: The 'creamy layer' concept was first introduced by the Supreme Court in the Indra Sawhney case (1992) for OBC reservations.

articles.headline: India Launches National AI Governance Framework 2.0
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) launched the National AI Governance Framework 2.0, aiming to ensure ethical, responsible, and secure development and deployment of Artificial Intelligence.
• The updated framework emphasizes a 'data-centric' approach, focusing on data privacy, security, and algorithmic fairness.
• It proposes the establishment of an AI Regulatory Authority to oversee compliance and foster innovation within ethical boundaries.
• Key principles include transparency, accountability, human oversight, and explainability in AI systems.
• The framework seeks to position India as a global leader in responsible AI development, balancing technological advancement with societal well-being.

articles.headline: India's Tourism Sector Records Record Growth, Boosted by Domestic Travel and Digital Initiatives
articles.explainer:
• India's tourism sector has witnessed unprecedented growth, with both domestic and international tourist arrivals reaching record highs in 2025.
• This surge is attributed to government initiatives like 'Dekho Apna Desh', improved infrastructure, and enhanced digital connectivity for travel planning and booking.
• The Ministry of Tourism highlighted the significant contribution of spiritual tourism, adventure tourism, and wellness tourism to this growth.
• The sector's expansion has led to substantial job creation and boosted local economies, especially in remote and rural areas.
• Efforts are underway to further streamline visa processes and promote India as a global MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions) destination.

articles.headline: India and Pakistan Hold Technical Talks on Resumption of Cross-Border Trade
articles.explainer:
• Technical delegations from India and Pakistan held discussions on modalities for the potential resumption of cross-border trade, particularly through the Wagah-Attari border.
• The talks focused on addressing logistical challenges, standardizing customs procedures, and ensuring security protocols for trade routes.
• This dialogue follows a period of strained relations, with trade largely suspended since 2019 following India's revocation of Article 370 in Jammu & Kashmir.
• While no immediate breakthrough was announced, both sides expressed cautious optimism regarding future engagement on trade and humanitarian issues.
• The discussions are seen as a confidence-building measure, potentially paving the way for broader diplomatic engagement.

articles.headline: National Mission for Clean Ganga Reports Significant Progress in River Rejuvenation
articles.explainer:
• The National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) released its annual report, highlighting significant strides in wastewater treatment, solid waste management, and biodiversity conservation along the Ganga river basin.
• Over 200 projects, including new Sewage Treatment Plants (STPs) and rehabilitation of existing ones, have been completed, leading to a measurable improvement in water quality at various stretches.
• Community participation and public awareness campaigns under the 'Namami Gange' program have been crucial in driving behavioral change.
• The report also noted progress in afforestation efforts and the conservation of aquatic biodiversity, particularly the Ganges River Dolphin.
• Challenges remain, including industrial pollution and agricultural runoff, requiring continued focus and inter-ministerial coordination.

articles.headline: G20 Report Highlights India's Digital Public Infrastructure as Global Model
articles.explainer:
• A new report published by the G20 working group on digital transformation lauded India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) as a successful model for developing nations.
• The report specifically praised the India Stack, comprising Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, for its role in fostering financial inclusion, digital payments, and transparent governance.
• It emphasized how India's DPI approach has enabled rapid scaling of digital services and reduced transaction costs across various sectors.
• Member nations were encouraged to explore adapting elements of India's DPI strategy to address their own digital divide and achieve sustainable development goals.
• The recognition strengthens India's position as a leader in digital innovation and a proponent of open-source digital solutions.

articles.headline: Indian Start-up Develops Advanced Indigenous Anti-Drone System for Critical Infrastructure Protection
articles.explainer:
• An Indian deep-tech start-up has successfully developed and demonstrated an advanced indigenous anti-drone system, marking a significant boost to national security capabilities.
• The system integrates AI-powered detection, tracking, and neutralization capabilities against various types of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), including swarm drones.
• It offers multi-layered protection for critical infrastructure, strategic installations, and public gatherings against potential drone threats.
• The development aligns with the 'Make in India' and 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives, reducing reliance on foreign technology for defense solutions.
• Government agencies have expressed keen interest in deploying the system across sensitive locations, enhancing India's defense preparedness against evolving aerial threats.

articles.headline: Report Highlights Progress and Gaps in Forest Rights Act Implementation
articles.explainer:
• A new report by a parliamentary committee reviewed the implementation of the Forest Rights Act (FRA), 2006, highlighting both significant progress and persistent challenges.
• The report noted that millions of individual and community forest rights titles have been recognized, empowering tribal communities and other forest dwellers.
• However, it also pointed out delays in processing claims, rejection of valid claims, and lack of awareness among beneficiaries in several states.
• Recommendations include streamlining the claim verification process, enhancing capacity building for local authorities, and ensuring convergence of FRA with other development schemes.
• The effective implementation of FRA is crucial for tribal welfare, biodiversity conservation, and achieving sustainable forest management.

articles.headline: Indian Agricultural Scientists Develop New Varieties of Drought-Resistant Crops
articles.explainer:
• Agricultural scientists at the Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) have announced the successful development of several new varieties of drought-resistant crops.
• These new strains, including specific varieties of wheat, rice, and millets, are genetically modified or selectively bred to withstand prolonged periods of water scarcity.
• The innovation aims to enhance food security, particularly in rain-fed agricultural regions prone to drought due to climate change.
• Farmers will benefit from increased yield stability, reduced crop failure risks, and improved income potential.
• The new varieties are expected to be available for cultivation after further field trials and regulatory approvals, representing a major step in climate-smart agriculture.

articles.headline: Editorial: Balancing Act - RBI's Prudent Stance on Inflation
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's decision to maintain the repo rate signals a cautious approach to monetary policy amidst global and domestic uncertainties.
• This editorial praises the RBI's commitment to inflation targeting while acknowledging the need to support economic growth.
• It argues that persistent food inflation and global supply chain disruptions necessitate a watchful stance, preventing premature easing of monetary policy.
• The article also calls for coordinated fiscal and supply-side measures from the government to complement the RBI's efforts in managing inflation.

articles.headline: India Accelerates Climate-Resilient Infrastructure Development in Coastal Regions
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change announced accelerated efforts to develop climate-resilient infrastructure in India's vulnerable coastal regions.
• This initiative involves implementing nature-based solutions, upgrading early warning systems, and constructing cyclone shelters and sea walls.
• The focus is on protecting coastal communities and ecosystems from the increasing threats of sea-level rise, extreme weather events, and coastal erosion.
• Projects are being undertaken under schemes like the National Coastal Mission and the Integrated Coastal Zone Management (ICZM) Program.
• The plan also includes capacity building for local communities to adapt to climate change impacts and sustainable livelihood diversification.

articles.headline: Review of National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Shows Mixed Progress
articles.explainer:
• A mid-term review of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation revealed mixed progress across various states and educational levels.
• While significant strides have been made in curriculum reforms, teacher training, and digital learning initiatives, challenges persist in infrastructure development and equity of access.
• The report highlighted successful adoption of foundational literacy and numeracy programs in some states, alongside slow progress in others due to funding and capacity issues.
• The Ministry of Education emphasized the need for greater inter-state collaboration, targeted funding, and continuous evaluation to achieve NEP's transformative goals.
• Stakeholders called for a more decentralized approach to implementation, allowing states to tailor reforms to their specific contexts.

articles.headline: India Rises in Global Start-up Ecosystem Rankings, Driven by Deep-Tech and Unicorns
articles.explainer:
• India has significantly climbed in the latest global start-up ecosystem rankings, solidifying its position among the top five nations globally.
• The rise is attributed to a booming deep-tech sector, increasing number of unicorns, and robust government support for innovation and entrepreneurship.
• Bengaluru, Delhi, and Mumbai continue to be major hubs, with emerging ecosystems in tier-2 and tier-3 cities also contributing to the growth.
• The report highlighted increased venture capital funding, a growing talent pool, and supportive policy frameworks as key drivers.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Circular Economy Policy 2025, consider the following statements:
1. It aims to shift India from a linear 'take-make-dispose' model to a restorative one.
2. The policy includes provisions for Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR).
3. It sets targets for waste reduction only in the plastics sector.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The policy aims for a restorative and regenerative model. Statement 2 is correct: Extended Producer Responsibility is a key pillar. Statement 3 is incorrect: The policy sets targets for waste reduction in key sectors like plastics, electronics, and construction & demolition waste, not just plastics.

prelimsMocks.question:

GS2.headline: Veteran Politician and Lawyer Swaraj Kaushal Passes Away at 73
GS2.explainer:
• Swaraj Kaushal, a distinguished lawyer and politician, passed away at the age of 73.
• He served as a Member of Parliament (MP) in the Rajya Sabha from 1998 to 2004, representing Haryana.
• Notably, he held the gubernatorial post as the Governor of Mizoram from 1990 to 1993, becoming one of India's youngest governors at the time.
• Kaushal was a prominent legal mind and was known for his constitutional expertise and parliamentary contributions.
• He was also the husband of late former External Affairs Minister Sushma Swaraj.

GS2.trivia: Swaraj Kaushal was involved in the peace talks with the Mizo National Front (MNF), which led to the Mizoram Peace Accord in 1986.

GS2.headline: India-ASEAN Maritime Security Dialogue Concludes with Focus on Indo-Pacific Stability
GS2.explainer:
• The 3rd India-ASEAN Maritime Security Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, emphasizing enhanced cooperation for regional stability and freedom of navigation in the Indo-Pacific.
• Delegates discussed joint efforts in counter-piracy, search and rescue operations, maritime domain awareness, and combating illegal, unreported, and unregulated (IUU) fishing.
• India reiterated its commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, aligning with its 'Act East' policy and Security and Growth for All in the Region (SAGAR) vision.
• The dialogue also explored avenues for capacity building and technology sharing among ASEAN member states and India.
• A joint statement highlighted the importance of adhering to international law, particularly UNCLOS, for resolving maritime disputes peacefully.

GS2.headline: Parliament Passes Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Bill, 2025
GS2.explainer:
• The Parliament successfully passed the Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Bill, 2025, which seeks to decriminalize minor offenses across various laws.
• The bill aims to promote ease of doing business and ease of living by replacing imprisonment with monetary penalties for several petty infractions.
• It proposes amendments to over 42 central Acts administered by 19 Ministries/Departments, including environmental laws, agriculture, and intellectual property.
• The move is intended to reduce the burden on the judicial system and encourage compliance by shifting from punitive to a more facilitative regulatory regime.
• The Bill also introduces a system of adjudicating officers for these monetary penalties.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Constitutional Validity of Reservations in Promotions for SC/STs
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark judgment, reaffirmed the constitutional validity of providing reservations in promotions for Scheduled Castes (SCs) and Scheduled Tribes (STs) in government jobs.
• The five-judge Constitution Bench clarified that while states are not bound to provide reservations in promotions, if they choose to do so, they must collect quantifiable data demonstrating the inadequacy of representation of SC/STs.
• The Court reiterated that the 'creamy layer' concept is not applicable to SC/ST reservations, distinguishing it from OBC reservations.
• This ruling provides much-needed clarity on the contentious issue, aligning with previous judgments while emphasizing the need for data-backed policy decisions.
• The judgment underscores the state's power to make special provisions for the advancement of socially and educationally backward classes.

GS2.headline: India and Pakistan Hold Technical Talks on Resumption of Cross-Border Trade
GS2.explainer:
• Technical delegations from India and Pakistan held discussions on modalities for the potential resumption of cross-border trade, particularly through the Wagah-Attari border.
• The talks focused on addressing logistical challenges, standardizing customs procedures, and ensuring security protocols for trade routes.
• This dialogue follows a period of strained relations, with trade largely suspended since 2019 following India's revocation of Article 370 in Jammu & Kashmir.
• While no immediate breakthrough was announced, both sides expressed cautious optimism regarding future engagement on trade and humanitarian issues.
• The discussions are seen as a confidence-building measure, potentially paving the way for broader diplomatic engagement.

GS2.headline: Report Highlights Progress and Gaps in Forest Rights Act Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• A new report by a parliamentary committee reviewed the implementation of the Forest Rights Act (FRA), 2006, highlighting both significant progress and persistent challenges.
• The report noted that millions of individual and community forest rights titles have been recognized, empowering tribal communities and other forest dwellers.
• However, it also pointed out delays in processing claims, rejection of valid claims, and lack of awareness among beneficiaries in several states.
• Recommendations include streamlining the claim verification process, enhancing capacity building for local authorities, and ensuring convergence of FRA with other development schemes.
• The effective implementation of FRA is crucial for tribal welfare, biodiversity conservation, and achieving sustainable forest management.

GS3.headline: India Unveils National Circular Economy Policy 2025
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the comprehensive National Circular Economy Policy 2025, aiming to transition India from a linear 'take-make-dispose' model to a restorative and regenerative one.
• The policy focuses on reducing waste generation, maximizing resource utilization, and fostering innovation in recycling and reuse technologies.
• Key pillars include extended producer responsibility, development of material recovery facilities, and promotion of eco-design principles across industries.
• It sets ambitious targets for waste reduction in key sectors like plastics, electronics, and construction & demolition waste by 2030.
• Financial incentives and regulatory frameworks are proposed to encourage businesses and consumers to adopt circular practices.

GS3.headline: RBI Keeps Repo Rate Unchanged, Focuses on Inflation Management
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
• This decision comes amidst persistent concerns over retail inflation, which remains above the MPC's comfort zone, despite easing global commodity prices.
• RBI Governor emphasized the need for continued vigilance on inflation, citing potential risks from volatile food prices and global supply chain disruptions.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Economic growth projections were maintained, with robust domestic demand seen as a key driver.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology Demonstrator
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful atmospheric flight test of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator.
• This critical test validates key technologies for an orbital reusable launch vehicle, including autonomous navigation, guidance, and control, as well as hypersonic flight capabilities.
• The RLV-TD program aims to significantly reduce the cost of space access by developing a two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) reusable launch system.
• The demonstrator, named 'Pushpak', performed a precise landing at a designated airstrip after being released from an Indian Air Force helicopter at an altitude of 4.5 km.
• This achievement marks a major step towards India's future space missions, including human spaceflight and advanced satellite deployments.

GS3.headline: India's Solar Energy Capacity Crosses 150 GW Mark, Pushing Towards Green Goals
GS3.explainer:
• India's total installed solar power capacity has officially surpassed the 150 GW milestone, marking a significant achievement in its renewable energy transition.
• This includes utility-scale solar projects, rooftop solar installations, and off-grid solar solutions, demonstrating a diversified growth strategy.
• The surge in capacity is attributed to supportive government policies, incentives for solar manufacturing, and declining costs of solar technology.
• This development brings India closer to its ambitious target of 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030, pledged at COP26.
• The growth in solar energy is also contributing to energy security, reduced carbon emissions, and job creation in the green sector.

GS3.headline: India Launches National AI Governance Framework 2.0
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) launched the National AI Governance Framework 2.0, aiming to ensure ethical, responsible, and secure development and deployment of Artificial Intelligence.
• The updated framework emphasizes a 'data-centric' approach, focusing on data privacy, security, and algorithmic fairness.
• It proposes the establishment of an AI Regulatory Authority to oversee compliance and foster innovation within ethical boundaries.
• Key principles include transparency, accountability, human oversight, and explainability in AI systems.
• The framework seeks to position India as a global leader in responsible AI development, balancing technological advancement with societal well-being.

GS3.headline: India's Tourism Sector Records Record Growth, Boosted by Domestic Travel and Digital Initiatives
GS3.explainer:
• India's tourism sector has witnessed unprecedented growth, with both domestic and international tourist arrivals reaching record highs in 2025.
• This surge is attributed to government initiatives like 'Dekho Apna Desh', improved infrastructure, and enhanced digital connectivity for travel planning and booking.
• The Ministry of Tourism highlighted the significant contribution of spiritual tourism, adventure tourism, and wellness tourism to this growth.
• The sector's expansion has led to substantial job creation and boosted local economies, especially in remote and rural areas.
• Efforts are underway to further streamline visa processes and promote India as a global MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions) destination.

GS3.headline: National Mission for Clean Ganga Reports Significant Progress in River Rejuvenation
GS3.explainer:
• The National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) released its annual report, highlighting significant strides in wastewater treatment, solid waste management, and biodiversity conservation along the Ganga river basin.
• Over 200 projects, including new Sewage Treatment Plants (STPs) and rehabilitation of existing ones, have been completed, leading to a measurable improvement in water quality at various stretches.
• Community participation and public awareness campaigns under the 'Namami Gange' program have been crucial in driving behavioral change.
• The report also noted progress in afforestation efforts and the conservation of aquatic biodiversity, particularly the Ganges River Dolphin.
• Challenges remain, including industrial pollution and agricultural runoff, requiring continued focus and inter-ministerial coordination.

GS3.headline: G20 Report Highlights India's Digital Public Infrastructure as Global Model
GS3.explainer:
• A new report published by the G20 working group on digital transformation lauded India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) as a successful model for developing nations.
• The report specifically praised the India Stack, comprising Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, for its role in fostering financial inclusion, digital payments, and transparent governance.
• It emphasized how India's DPI approach has enabled rapid scaling of digital services and reduced transaction costs across various sectors.
• Member nations were encouraged to explore adapting elements of India's DPI strategy to address their own digital divide and achieve sustainable development goals.
• The recognition strengthens India's position as a leader in digital innovation and a proponent of open-source digital solutions.

GS3.headline: Indian Start-up Develops Advanced Indigenous Anti-Drone System for Critical Infrastructure Protection
GS3.explainer:
• An Indian deep-tech start-up has successfully developed and demonstrated an advanced indigenous anti-drone system, marking a significant boost to national security capabilities.
• The system integrates AI-powered detection, tracking, and neutralization capabilities against various types of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), including swarm drones.
• It offers multi-layered protection for critical infrastructure, strategic installations, and public gatherings against potential drone threats.
• The development aligns with the 'Make in India' and 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives, reducing reliance on foreign technology for defense solutions.
• Government agencies have expressed keen interest in deploying the system across sensitive locations, enhancing India's defense preparedness against evolving aerial threats.

GS3.headline: Government Announces New Policy to Boost Medical Devices Manufacturing in India
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Health Ministry unveiled a new comprehensive policy aimed at accelerating domestic manufacturing of medical devices and reducing import dependence.
• The policy introduces production-linked incentive (PLI) schemes, tax benefits, and easier regulatory approvals for manufacturers.
• It focuses on fostering innovation, research & development, and creating dedicated medical device parks with world-class infrastructure.
• The move is expected to attract significant investment, create skilled jobs, and make healthcare more affordable and accessible in India.
• Emphasis is also placed on quality standards and global competitiveness to establish India as a hub for medical device exports.

GS3.headline: Indian Agricultural Scientists Develop New Varieties of Drought-Resistant Crops
GS3.explainer:
• Agricultural scientists at the Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) have announced the successful development of several new varieties of drought-resistant crops.
• These new strains, including specific varieties of wheat, rice, and millets, are genetically modified or selectively bred to withstand prolonged periods of water scarcity.
• The innovation aims to enhance food security, particularly in rain-fed agricultural regions prone to drought due to climate change.
• Farmers will benefit from increased yield stability, reduced crop failure risks, and improved income potential.
• The new varieties are expected to be available for cultivation after further field trials and regulatory approvals, representing a major step in climate-smart agriculture.

GS3.headline: Editorial: Balancing Act - RBI's Prudent Stance on Inflation
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's decision to maintain the repo rate signals a cautious approach to monetary policy amidst global and domestic uncertainties.
• This editorial praises the RBI's commitment to inflation targeting while acknowledging the need to support economic growth.
• It argues that persistent food inflation and global supply chain disruptions necessitate a watchful stance, preventing premature easing of monetary policy.
• The article also calls for coordinated fiscal and supply-side measures from the government to complement the RBI's efforts in managing inflation.
• It suggests that long-term structural reforms are essential to build resilience against inflationary pressures and ensure sustainable economic expansion.

GS3.headline: India Accelerates Climate-Resilient Infrastructure Development in Coastal Regions
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change announced accelerated efforts to develop climate-resilient infrastructure in India's vulnerable coastal regions.
• This initiative involves implementing nature-based solutions, upgrading early warning systems, and constructing cyclone shelters and sea walls.
• The focus is on protecting coastal communities and ecosystems from the increasing threats of sea-level rise, extreme weather events, and coastal erosion.
• Projects are being undertaken under schemes like the National Coastal Mission and the Integrated Coastal Zone Management (ICZM) Program.
• The plan also includes capacity building for local communities to adapt to climate change impacts and sustainable livelihood diversification.

GS3.headline: India Rises in Global Start-up Ecosystem Rankings, Driven by Deep-Tech and Unicorns
GS3.explainer:
• India has significantly climbed in the latest global start-up ecosystem rankings, solidifying its position among the top five nations globally.
• The rise is attributed to a booming deep-tech sector, increasing number of unicorns, and robust government support for innovation and entrepreneurship.
• Bengaluru, Delhi, and Mumbai continue to be major hubs, with emerging ecosystems in tier-2 and tier-3 cities also contributing to the growth.
• The report highlighted increased venture capital funding, a growing talent pool, and supportive policy frameworks as key drivers.
• This recognition underscores India's potential to become a global innovation powerhouse and a significant contributor to the digital economy.
